title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony Kidd meets with a group of officials, including Chief Inspector Bartlett, Commissioner Mallow, and George Roxanne, to explain his presence at the Earl of Carringham's estate. Anthony admits he broke into the estate at approximately 11:40 the previous night because he heard gunshots. He explains that he came to clear his name after realizing his boots had been taken by a policeman, making him a suspect.

Anthony provides a detailed backstory, claiming he traveled from Bulawayo to England under the alias "Jimmy McGregor" to perform a favor for a friend. He states he checked into the Britz Hotel using this name to deliver a manuscript to a publisher. He claims he received a letter from Mr. Roxanne (whom he refers to as Mr. Roxby or Roxemax) regarding the manuscript and an invitation to a banquet at the Earl's residence. Anthony asserts that he had actually declined the invitation, but decided to visit the estate late at night to check on things due to concerns regarding "Chimney House" and a man named Gisippus. He describes hearing a gunshot while approaching the dark house, which led him to return to an inn for the night.

During the questioning, George Roxanne becomes visibly uncomfortable when Anthony mentions the name Jimmy McGregor. George reveals that the document in question was a letter containing the memoirs of the late Count Stapleton. Finally, George reluctantly discloses that the man shot at the estate, who had been disguised as Count Stanislaus, was actually Prince Michael of Hesse. Chief Inspector Bartle observes Anthony's testimony with apparent satisfaction.
